repair_instructions: Add a clearly visible bulletin board / notice area with pinned student work and labels on a wall (ensure it is readable at oblique view distance).
Place a clearly recognizable classroom wall clock (large face, readable hands/numbers) near the teaching wall.
Add at least one visible student backpack (e.g., hung on a chair back or placed near cubbies) without blocking aisles.
Add a distinct storage cabinet with doors (not just open cubbies) and place it visibly in-frame.
Make required small items unambiguous and visible: whiteboard eraser on the tray/ledge, visible worksheets/textbooks on student desks, and visible storage bins/trays.
Establish a stronger front teaching wall (wall-mounted board or clearly designated front) and rotate/reposition student desks so the seating orientation is clearly toward the board.
Ensure at least one doorway is visible in a review view (or reposition camera) so door clearance can be verified.
Raise these combined scores to the pass threshold: object_relevance, clearance_and_access, prompt_alignment
room_prompt:
[SCENESMITH_SCHOOL_CONTRACT_V1 room_id=classroom_03 profile=school_reference_20260710]
Immutable effective prompt SHA-256: ac8d297cc9a2d605f41b4bcd7abd52aac29bfd0f195875840342ee1e6a7da86f

Use warm cream/beige/off-white walls, light wood furniture and flooring, soft daylight, warm ceiling light, organized school displays, realistic scale, greenery, and clear humanoid/robot circulation. Nothing may float, visibly interpenetrate, block a door, or eliminate a usable walking/turning path.

ROOM-SPECIFIC REQUIRED CONTENTS:
Create exactly 12 student desks and exactly 12 corresponding student chairs. Orient them toward a strong front teaching wall/whiteboard with clear aisles. Include a teacher desk and teacher chair, storage cabinet, bookshelf or cubbies, bulletin board/educational posters, classroom clock, trash bin, at least one plant, and visible exterior daylight/windows. Add believable school-use details including textbooks, notebooks, folders/worksheets, pencil/marker holders, erasers/rulers, glue/scissors, water bottles, backpacks, storage bins and paper trays without clutter. Make the room active but organized, with grouped desks, open cubbies, colorful student work, classroom bins, and a wall notice area.

--- FLOOR-PLANNER CONTEXT (SECONDARY) ---
Reference west-wing middle classroom with active learning clusters.

View 2 shows a teacher desk with organized supplies (paper trays, pencil cup, scissors, notebooks), suggesting an active-but-organized intent.
View 3 shows windows and wall posters plus open cubbies, but several contract-required items are not clearly identifiable (clock, bulletin board, backpack, distinct storage cabinet).

collision_free_support.observation: Furniture and small objects appear supported on the floor/desktops with no obvious floating or severe interpenetration visible.

daylight_and_warm_lighting.observation: Windows are visible and the room is lit with a warm overall tone consistent with daylight + warm interior illumination.
